notes: Forward returns at 5/20/60d horizons overlap across consecutive daily signals; effective independent sample is far below nominal n. Directional evidence only — not grounds for mechanical parameter changes.
v8 telemetry (divergence, hostile_review, regime driver/basis) began accruing mid-June 2026; sections 2-4 deepen with time.
Temporal z baseline excludes the most recent observation; generate-signals consumes the stored baseline next morning (one-day staleness, immaterial at window 60).

tactical.falsifier: A sustained daily close back above SMA50 ($300) with RSI clearing 50 would flip this from oversold-buy to momentum-neutral/short; conversely a fresh 52w low below $260 with accelerating volume confirms the downtrend is not exhausted.

positional.falsifier: A book-to-bill print below 0.95 with negative backlog YoY on the next earnings call flips this positive (trim); conversely confirmed B/B >1.10 with margin recovery toward 12%+ deepens the buy.

strategic.falsifier: A shift in DoD budget posture toward sequester/caps or a clear peace-dividend regime signal (major conflict de-escalation) flips this decisively to trim; conversely confirmation the margin dip is a one-time charge with backlog intact turns it to a buy.

tactical.falsifier: A two-day reversal giving back the entire +3.68% and RSI dropping below 45 would flip this to a modest buy-the-dip signal; conversely RSI spiking >75 on continued melt-up would push toward trim.

positional.falsifier: A drawdown of >15% from highs (below ~$1480) would flip to a buy; a break below the 200 SMA ($1365) on capex-cut news would confirm trim.

strategic.falsifier: Book-to-bill dropping below 1.0 plus explicit capex cuts from TSMC AND Samsung AND Intel simultaneously would confirm/raise the trim; conversely a >15% drawdown or forward P/E compressing below 25x on strong orders would flip this to a buy.

tactical.falsifier: RSI breaking below 35 on accelerating volume with LIN decisively losing the $472 200-day support would flip this to a clear positive (trim) tactical read.

positional.falsifier: BBB OAS blowing out past ~140bps combined with LIN breaking below the 200-day ($472) would flip this positive as the capex-IRR backlog tailwind reverses into a headwind.

strategic.falsifier: Confirmation that ROCE/op-margin compression is structural (moat erosion) AND price/mix-ex-FX turning negative would deepen the trim; conversely, a >$1B green/blue H2 offtake win or clear semi fab on-site contract cluster would flip strategic to negative (buy).

tactical.falsifier: A break below the $349 52w low on heavy volume, or Azure CC guidance cut below 22%, would confirm the downtrend is fundamental — flipping tactical to sell/neutral.

positional.falsifier: Azure CC growth decelerating below 22% with capex still rising (thesis impairment) would flip this to neutral/sell; conversely a decisive reclaim of the 200-day would justify pushing back toward the engine's -81.

strategic.falsifier: OpenAI partnership unwind/adverse renegotiation (loss of IP access/exclusivity) or forward P/E pushing above 35x on stalling Azure would flip strategic to sell.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive RSI move below 30 (oversold panic on a rate spike) would flip this to a tactical buy; an RSI push above 70 into fresh highs would justify a tactical trim.

positional.falsifier: The 10Y falling toward ~4.1% (widening spread back above ~75bps) while ENB holds, or a fresh accretive LNG/expansion contract, would flip this to a buy.

strategic.falsifier: A sustained drop in long rates (10Y toward 4%) restoring a normal ~100bps+ yield spread, or a materially accretive new LNG/expansion sanction, would flip strategic to buy; conversely a distributable-cash-flow coverage breach threatening the dividend would deepen the trim.

tactical.falsifier: A daily close back above the 50-day SMA ($14.83) on rising volume would flip this to a buy-lean.

positional.falsifier: COPX rolling over (copper price breaking down) while GLNCY holds would remove the catch-up thesis and flip this toward neutral/trim.

strategic.falsifier: LME copper breaking sustainably below ~$4.00/lb alongside a Chinese PMI relapse below 48 would signal the trough is deepening, flipping strategic to trim.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive break and close below the $32.84 52w low on rising volume would flip tactical positive (trim/sell).

strategic.falsifier: Sustained multi-month ETF net outflows combined with LTH mass distribution would flip strategic to a clear sell; accelerating institutional adoption would push it clearly negative.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive close below the 200-day SMA ($99) on rising volume, or MXN breaking past ~18.5, would flip this positive (trim).

positional.falsifier: A margin miss or volume decline in the next quarterly report, or MXN weakening sharply toward 18.5+, would flip this to a trim.

tactical.falsifier: A close above the 50-day SMA (~$18.11) on strong volume would flip me negative (buy); a sharp WTI drop below $72 or a hostile CEO/pricing headline would flip me positive.

positional.falsifier: A break below the $81.24 52w low, or confirmation of cRPO decelerating below 18% at next earnings — would remove the positional buy thesis and flip toward neutral.

strategic.falsifier: cRPO decelerating below 18% with no offsetting agentic-AI monetization inflection, OR real rate pushing sustainably above 2% — would flip strategic to a genuine positive/trim score. Conversely a cRPO re-acceleration + Fed pivot would flip it clearly negative/buy.

tactical.falsifier: Drawdown deepening past -12% to -15% (break below SMA200 $528 with RSI rolling under 45) would flip this to a tactical buy; conversely a blow-off to RSI >65 near $580+ would flip to mild trim.

positional.falsifier: Cross-border volume printing <8% or negative 30/90d EPS revisions at the next report would flip this positive (trim); a strong beat with cross-border >14% sustained deepens the buy.

tactical.falsifier: If the July 16 print (or subsequent commentary) confirmed measurable US procedure-share loss to Hugo OR quantified the 2027 transition as material (>mid-single-digit % of revenue), the drawdown is justified erosion and the tactical score flips positive.

positional.falsifier: If 30d/90d EPS revisions turn sharply negative confirming analysts are cutting numbers (not just multiple), OR US procedure-share loss is confirmed, the 'cheap' thesis breaks and the score moves toward neutral/positive.
